---
title: "MP29 hockeystick candidate"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">MP29: All-index Hockeystick Candidate</div>

::: {.status-note}
**Status:** advanced candidate in the current `jmMSE` workflow. MP29 is not
documented here as adopted. This page reflects `jmMSE` commit
`94d4d325ba46220f56097b3de90824c83b7cf3cd` and the 25 July 2026 presentation.
:::

## Identity

| Component | Current definition |
|---|---|
| `jmMSE` run | `tun29`, labelled MP29 |
| Estimator | `cpues.ind` |
| Indices | `Chile_AcousN`, `Chile_CPUE`, `Peru_Artis`, `Peru_Ind`, `Offshore_CPUE` |
| Reference years | 2019-2023 |
| Recent-data window | 3 years |
| Combination and metric | `combine = "mean"`, `metric = "mean"` |
| HCR | `hockeystick.hcr`; `hockeystick.hcr_rob` in multi-stock robustness runs |
| Output | Catch |
| Implementation | `split.is`, using last-five-year catch proportions |

## HCR parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---:|
| Minimum catch, `min` | 270 |
| Limit metric, `lim` | 0.1 |
| Trigger metric | 2.12 |
| Target catch | 2000 |
| Initial catch for constraints | 1385 |
| Maximum annual decrease multiplier, `dlow` | 0.85 |
| Maximum annual increase multiplier, `dupp` | 1.20 |

The trigger is the tuned value reported in the July presentation. Before
formal use it should be read from a frozen control object or normative registry,
not reconstructed by rerunning the tuner.

## Rule

Let $I_t$ be the annual `mean` metric returned by the specified estimator. The
unconstrained catch recommendation $C_t^*$ is

$$
C_t^* =
\begin{cases}
270, & I_t \le 0.1,\\
270 + (I_t-0.1)\dfrac{2000-270}{2.12-0.1}, & 0.1 < I_t < 2.12,\\
2000, & I_t \ge 2.12.
\end{cases}
$$

The interannual constraint limits the recommendation to 85%-120% of the
previous recommendation. In the first application year the current code uses
1385 as the previous value.

## Evidence status

The July presentation identifies MP29 as the optimal hockeystick minimum/limit
run and reports lower IACC than MP32, with slightly higher short-term catches.
`model_robustness.R` advances MP29 alongside MP32 across robustness OMs. Final
adoption still requires complete, reproducible robustness results and an
approved operational data contract.

Primary sources: [`model_tune.R`](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE/blob/main/model_tune.R),
[`model_robustness.R`](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE/blob/main/model_robustness.R),
and the [July presentation source](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE/blob/main/doc/presentation/presentation_20260725.Rmd).

## Annual application requirements still to resolve

- authoritative provider and annual cutoff for each index;
- precise standardization performed by `cpues.ind` and its package version;
- treatment of missing or revised indices;
- catch units and rounding;
- approved catch-allocation update rule; and
- exceptional-circumstances review and decision process.
